data.description:
## Context / Problem

`criterion_prompt_id` â€” the single logicalâ†’physical criterion-id map both review gates
delegate to â€” hardcodes the plan-review prompt prefix (`PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "plan-review-"`
at `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:31`, applied unconditionally at `ids.py:42`). So a project
**code-review** criterion resolves into the plan-review prompt namespace, the first of five seams
that drop project code-review criteria (root cause on superseded bug 8a4b). This is the shared
abstraction the other four seams consume, so it lands first.

## Approach

Make the resolver gate-aware, additively:

- Add a keyword-only `gate_key: str = "plan_review"` parameter to
  `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, *, gate_key="plan_review")`.
- Introduce a `_PROMPT_PREFIX = {"plan_review": "plan-review-", "code_review": "code-review-"}`
  map in `ids.py`; select the prefix by `gate_key`; an unknown `gate_key` raises `CriteriaError`
  (imported from `rebar.llm.criteria.model`, confirmed present at
  `src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py`).
- The `.`â†’`-` namespace rewrite is unchanged, so `project.foo` + `code_review` â†’
  `code-review-project-foo` and stays filesystem-safe and injective per the existing charset
  invariant documented in the module docstring.

The default `"plan_review"` preserves today's behavior byte-for-byte, so the two existing callers
(`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302`, `src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487`)
need **no** change and plan-review parity is guaranteed by construction. Code-review call sites
(introduced by the fan-in and eval children) pass `gate_key="code_review"` explicitly.

**Rejected alternatives.** (a) Making `gate_key` *required* â€” it would force editing
`eval.py:419`'s existing call, colliding with the eval-admission child's file set and breaking the
disjoint-file decomposition; the default is the historically-correct value, not a silent bug. (b)
Registering the prefix on the gate via `criteria.overlay.register_gate` â€” adds import-order
coupling to gate registration for no caller benefit; the forward-only map is the pattern the
module docstring already commits to.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` â€” add `gate_key` param + `_PROMPT_PREFIX` map.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` â€” NEW file; the shared test file later
  children extend. This child adds the resolver unit tests only.

## Testing

- Happy path: `criterion_prompt_id("project.foo", gate_key="code_review") == "code-review-project-foo"`;
  `criterion_prompt_id("F1", gate_key="plan_review") == "plan-review-F1"`.
- Parity/default: `criterion_prompt_id("F1") == "plan-review-F1"` (no `gate_key` given) â€” existing
  plan-review behavior unchanged.
- Edge: an unknown `gate_key` raises `CriteriaError`; cross-gate injectivity â€” no code-review
  project id collides with any plan-review physical id.

Tier: unit (pure function, no I/O).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `criterion_prompt_id("project.foo", gate_key="code_review")` returns
      `"code-review-project-foo"`; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k resolver`.
- [ ] `criterion_prompt_id("F1")` (no gate_key) still returns `"plan-review-F1"`, proving
      plan-review parity; verified by the same `-k resolver` selector.
- [ ] An unknown `gate_key` raises `CriteriaError`; verified by the same `-k resolver` selector.
data.title: Gate-key the criterion-id resolver (criterion_prompt_id)
